title: onosma frutescens
intro: Onosma frutescens, also known as Hook's onosma, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of alkaloids, including frutescine and onosmin, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Onosma frutescens include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Onosma frutescens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for a range of health concerns.
